headline: Quantum stocks surge as US govt. invests $2B, takes equity stakes
summary: Quantum computing stocks IBM (IBM), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), and Infleqtion (INFQ) — the latter three are all up by over 40% over the last five sessions — after the US Commerce Department announced it will award $2 billion in grants and take equity stakes in these companies. Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman breaks down take the details of the US government's investment in these firms, citing comments from IBM's vice president of quantum adoption Scott Crowder. She also uses AlphaSpace — Yahoo Finance's new investing platform — to take a closer look at the quantum landscape. Earlier this week, Yahoo Finance launched AlphaSpace, a premium investment platform that combines data, charting, news, analysis, and more to help retail investors understand markets.

content.title: Quantum stocks surge as US govt. invests $2B, takes equity stakes
content.description:
Quantum computing stocks IBM (IBM), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), and Infleqtion (INFQ) — the latter three are all up by over 40% over the last five sessions — after the US Commerce Department announced it will award $2 billion in grants and take equity stakes in these companies.

Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman breaks down take the details of the US government's investment in these firms, citing comments from IBM's vice president of quantum adoption Scott Crowder. She also uses AlphaSpace — Yahoo Finance's new investing platform — to take a closer look at the quantum landscape.

Earlier this week, Yahoo Finance launched AlphaSpace, a premium investment platform that combines data, charting, news, analysis, and more to help retail investors understand markets.
content.summary: Quantum computing stocks IBM (IBM), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), and Infleqtion (INFQ) — the latter three are all up by over 40% over the last five sessions — after the US Commerce Department announced it will award $2 billion in grants and take equity stakes in these companies. Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman breaks down take the details of the US government's investment in these firms, citing comments from IBM's vice president of quantum adoption Scott Crowder. She also uses AlphaSpace — Yahoo Finance's new investing platform — to take a closer look at the quantum landscape. Earlier this week, Yahoo Finance launched AlphaSpace, a premium investment platform that combines data, charting, news, analysis, and more to help retail investors understand markets.

headline: What's Happening With IBM Stock?
summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) surged 12.4% to close at $252.97 on May 21, 2026, after the Commerce Department announced $2.0 billion in quantum computing grants to nine companies. The headline? IBM secures $1 billion in government funding for a new quantum chip foundry called Anderon, with IBM matching the investment dollar-for-dollar. The stock's reaction was swift and decisive.

headline: Commerce Department announces $2B for 9 companies under CHIPS Act
summary: The funds will support GlobalFoundries and IBM, along with seven quantum computing companies, in their efforts to develop “utility-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computers,” the DOC said.

content.title: Commerce Department announces $2B for 9 companies under CHIPS Act
content.summary: The funds will support GlobalFoundries and IBM, along with seven quantum computing companies, in their efforts to develop “utility-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computers,” the DOC said.

thumbnail.caption: The Herbert C. Hoover Building is in Washington, DC, is the headquarters for the United States Department of Commerce. The agency will issue antidumping and countervailing duty orders for polypropylene corrugated boxes from China after the U.S. International Trade Commission determined in February that these imports harm U.S. businesses.
